text.title: Naming and Domains Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Naming and Domains application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Names MUST be pronounceable in each target locale (test by reading to one native speaker per locale). Unpronounceable candidates are dropped.
rule.rationale: Unpronounceable names lose recall and word-of-mouth referrals.

rule.statement: Top-3 finalists are checked against the USPTO TESS, EUIPO, and a generic Google search. Any near-hit drops the candidate.
rule.rationale: Late-stage trademark collisions force rebrands and cost more than the upfront check.

rule.statement: Apex .com (or, fallback, .ai / .net) MUST be available or acquirable at <USD 1k. ccTLD-only fallback is allowed only if the brand is locale-bound.
rule.rationale: Subdomain or hyphenated alternates hurt SEO and trust.

rule.statement: Top-3 names are checked on X, GitHub, LinkedIn, and Bluesky. Unavailable handles drop the candidate unless the operator commits to a documented suffix (-app / -hq).
rule.rationale: Disjoint social handles fragment the brand at launch.

rule.statement: Final report ranks top 3 with explicit rationale per criterion (recall / trademark / domain / handles). Single-name reports are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-name reports hide the trade-offs and force later relitigation.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
